paper_title: Study of Aquatic Systems in Colombia

methods.description: Collection of samples from various aquatic systems in Colombia to study geographical and ecological characteristics.

steps.description: Samples were collected from lotic and lentic systems of the Departments of Antioquia and Santander, and from three shallow swamps in the Department of Chocó.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples from specified aquatic systems in Colombia

steps.description: Epiliton samples at La Vega Stream were collected between August 2001 and April 2002 at three sampling places, brushing a total area of 900 cm².
steps.searchable_summary: collect epiliton samples from La Vega Stream

steps.description: At the Lebrija River Basin, samples were collected during May and July 2000 by brushing the surfaces of a variable number of substrates delimited with a 25 cm² acrylic frame.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples from Lebrija River Basin using acrylic frame

steps.description: Fishes stomach contents samples from Tumaradó Swamps were obtained from Prochilodus magdalenae (Bocachico).
steps.searchable_summary: collect fish stomach content samples from Tumaradó Swamps

methods.description: Treatment of collected samples to eliminate organic matter and prepare them for microscopy analysis.

steps.description: Samples were fixed with 10% Lugol or 6-8% formalin and were treated to eliminate organic matter following the method described in CEN/TC 230 (2002).
steps.searchable_summary: fix samples with Lugol or formalin and treat to eliminate organic matter

steps.description: Samples for light microscopy (LM) were mounted in Naphrax and for scanning electron microscopy (SEM) on glass stubs and then coated with gold-palladium.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples for LM and SEM by mounting and coating

methods.description: Observation of prepared samples using light and scanning electron microscopy to analyze physical and chemical characteristics.

steps.description: Observations were carried out with a Wild M20 LM and a Jeol JSM-T100 SEM at the Servicio de Microscopía Electrónica del Museo de La Plata.
steps.searchable_summary: observe samples using Wild M20 LM and Jeol JSM-T100 SEM
